rulings.Case Title: LINDA CATRON VS. COUNTRYWIDE BANK, FSB ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Joinder And Joinder In Motion To Dismiss
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 15, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANTSS NOTICE OF JOINDER AND JOINDER IN MOTION TO DISMISS is continued to November 15, 2023. No later than November 1, 2023. Plaintiff to file an opposition. If opposition is not filed as ordered, the matter will be considered unopposed. The Court will not consider any filings after November 1, 2023. If opposition is timely filed and served, Defendant may file a reply no later than November 9, 2023. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PHUONG T. NGUYEN VS. LONDON BREED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 15, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Despite prior rulings concerning the 14th cause of action, Plaintiff failed to make the requisite allegations and opposition does not suggest that such allegations may be made if leave to amend is granted. The defendant must submit a proposed order in accordance with this tentative ruling at the hearing, The Court notes that the Defendants again failed to comply with CRC 3.1312; the Court's instructions on submissions of the orders after the hearing published on the Court's web-site; the Clerk's April 18, 2023 rejection letters; and the Court's August 7, 2023 ruling regarding orders on the prior demurrers. (Attached to the papers with a hearing date of September 15, 2023 provided by the Defendants the Court locates proposed orders for April 14, 2023 and September 20, 2021. These orders were not accompanied by any evidence of compliance with CRC 3.1312. Failure to provide such evidence was the reason for rejection on April 18, 2023) Therefore, in accordance with CRC 3.1312(d), the self-represented Plaintiff may submit orders re: April 14, 2023 and September 20, 2021 upon complying with CRC 3.1312. Orders must track the tentative rulings verbatim and must be accompanied by a cover letter attesting to compliance with CRC 3.1312(a) and (b). The Court notes that Plaintiff attempted to submit the order on or around August 11, 2023 but provided no proper information regarding compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SAAM BARRAGER ET AL VS. LAURA DOUGLASS DECARAVA
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 15, 2023 line 5. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. The filing does not include a motion (only notice of motion and points and authorities filed). Notice of motion contains no grounds for the motion. Points and authorities are oversized and do not contain Table of Contents or Table of Authorities. Motion is based on disputed issues of fact that are not a proper subject of a Motion to Strike. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
